steps.title: Substitute the resistance
steps.content: Use $R=18$ in the given relationship:$$\frac{CV^2}{P}=\sqrt{18P}$$The variables are positive, so the later squaring step will not introduce a sign issue for $P$.

steps.title: Eliminate the radical
steps.content: Square both sides and multiply by $P^2$:$$\frac{C^2V^4}{P^2}=18P$$$$C^2V^4=18P^3$$Then divide by $18$ to isolate $P^3$.

steps.title: Apply the cube root
steps.content: The result is $$P^3=\frac{C^2V^4}{18}$$$$P=\sqrt[3]{\frac{C^2V^4}{18}}$$This matches the expression written with a cube root. The answer is C.
